bead.title: Dashboard UI: cross-project workers view (all live workers across all projects on one screen)
bead.description:
When an operator has N projects in flight (e.g. ddx, axon, helix) the FEAT-021 routing puts each project's workers under /nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/agents. To see what's running across ALL projects you currently have to click into each project separately. Add a node-level (combined) view that lists every running worker on the node regardless of project, so the operator has one situational-awareness pane.

Backing data: GET /api/agent/workers already returns ALL workers managed by the server (it's a node-wide endpoint, not scoped to a single project). The UI just needs to render this list with a project column.

UI requirements:
- Route: /nodes/:nodeId/agents (combined; sibling of /nodes/:nodeId/beads and /nodes/:nodeId/sessions)
- Columns: project name, worker id, bead id, harness, model, state, started-at, attempts, last-status
- Auto-refresh every ~3s
- Each row links to /nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/agents/:workerId for the per-worker detail (which is ddx-7be82986)
- Project picker dropdown should also expose 'All projects' as a value that navigates to this page
- Empty-state message when zero workers are running
bead.acceptance: Playwright test seeds the server's worker registry with three workers belonging to three different project IDs (test fixture via direct WorkerManager API or stubbed /api/agent/workers response). Loads /nodes/:nodeId/agents. Asserts: (1) all three workers appear in the table, (2) each row has a non-empty 'Project' column matching the seeded project name, (3) row order is by started-at descending, (4) clicking a row navigates to /nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/agents/:workerId with the URL params matching the clicked worker's project and id, (5) when a worker's state transitions from running to exited the table updates within one refresh cycle. Backend Go test asserts GET /api/agent/workers returns workers from multiple projects in a single response array (already true today; lock it in).
